description: Cross-cutting taxonomies that Gunny uses to classify, build, and scale CrossFit programming.

energy_systems.definition: The three primary metabolic pathways used in CrossFit programming. OPEX uses the MAP (Maximal Aerobic Power) 1-10 framework as the most granular system.

time_domains.definition: Standard CrossFit time domain buckets used for benchmark classification and program balance.

strength_paradigms.definition: The most common strength programming frameworks used in CrossFit-adjacent methodologies.

paradigms.name: Linear Periodization
paradigms.description: Progressive load increase from high-rep/low-load to low-rep/high-load over a cycle. Common in beginner programs.

paradigms.name: Block Periodization
paradigms.description: Sequential focus blocks (accumulation → transmutation → realization). Each block emphasizes one quality.

paradigms.name: Concurrent Training
paradigms.description: Multiple qualities trained simultaneously in same week. CrossFit's GPP foundation.

paradigms.name: Conjugate Method
paradigms.description: Westside-derived. Max effort + dynamic effort + repetition method rotated. Adapted for CrossFit by some competitors.

paradigms.name: RPE / Autoregulated
paradigms.description: Loads selected by Rate of Perceived Exertion (1-10) rather than fixed percentages. Adjusts for daily readiness.

paradigms.name: Percentage of 1RM
paradigms.description: Loads prescribed as % of athlete's 1RM. Standard in HWPO, Mayhem, Misfit.

weekly_template_archetypes.definition: Common weekly structures used in CrossFit programming. Choice depends on athlete tier, recovery capacity, and goal.

templates.name: 5-on / 2-off
templates.description: Classic Mon-Fri training, weekend off. Best for beginner-intermediate, lifestyle-focused athletes.

templates.name: 6-day with 1 rest
templates.description: Six training days with one full rest. Most common for serious recreational + competitor athletes.

templates.name: 3-on / 1-off rolling
templates.description: Train 3 days, rest 1, repeat. ~22 sessions/month. High volume.

templates.name: A-week / B-week
templates.description: Two-week microcycle alternating focus. Reduces stale stimulus.

templates.name: Double-day competitor
templates.description: AM + PM sessions for elite athletes. Strength AM, conditioning PM (or vice versa).

scaling_principles.definition: How to scale benchmark workouts to preserve the intended stimulus.

principles.name: Preserve the Stimulus
principles.description: Scale to keep the workout's intended time domain and intensity. Fran should stay 3-7min for everyone.

principles.name: Scale Volume Before Load (typically)
principles.description: For long workouts, reduce reps before reducing weight. For short workouts, reduce weight before reducing reps.

principles.name: Preserve Movement Intent
principles.description: If a workout tests muscle-up cycling, sub jumping muscle-ups or banded MUs — not pull-ups + dips, which removes the transition.

principles.name: Tier-Appropriate Substitutions
principles.description: Pull-up → ring row (beginner), banded pull-up (intermediate), strict pull-up (advanced who lacks kip).

principles.name: Load Scaling
principles.description: Standard scaling drops: Rx → 80% Rx → 65% Rx → 50% Rx for foundations.

principles.name: Time Caps Drive Scaling
principles.description: If 80% of athletes won't finish in cap, scaling is too heavy/high-volume regardless of Rx label.

athlete_tiers.definition: Standard tier definitions used for programming decisions. Most methodologies use a 4-5 tier model.

tiers.name: Foundations / Beginner
characteristics: No double-unders, no muscle-ups, no kipping pull-ups
Bodyweight or empty bar most lifts

tiers.name: Intermediate
characteristics: Has DUs, kipping pull-ups, basic gymnastics
Can perform most movements at lighter Rx

tiers.name: Competitor / Regional-Quarterfinal
characteristics: Has 1RM benchmarks above 90% bodyweight class averages

tiers.name: Elite / Games
programming_focus: Sport-specific (lift specialty, gymnastics specialty, engine work)

movement_categorization.definition: How to classify movements for balanced programming. Aim for variety within and across these categories weekly.
movement_categorization.balance_rule: Weekly programming should hit all 5 categories. Most CrossFit methodologies aim for 3-4 weightlifting sessions, 3-4 gymnastics sessions, 3-5 monostructural pieces, 1-2 odd-object exposures per week.
